message: Construction rebuild stopped at an exact obstruction in the mandated v20 composite extension. The valid v18 two-node mirror Gaussian-ANM geometry, Chernoff frontier, local elbow, linear reduction, conditional common-map score-contrast consistency, and deterministic rare-event diagnostic are retained below. The requested field upgrade cannot be emitted soundly: a Gaussian-Hermite Sobolev ball with only s>2 has no uniform numerical multiplication/tail control, while adding the needed uniform control changes the high-frequency least-favourable set and therefore the claimed single C_m-to-C_star modulus and matching detector boundary. Stating the advertised modulus, mixture lower bound, or universal detector would assume the unresolved crux.

symbols.name: P
symbols.def: a positive twice continuously differentiable observational law with density p
symbols.role: generic law used to define the score functional

symbols.name: p
symbols.def: the Lebesgue density of the generic law P

symbols.name: (X,Y)
symbols.def: a draw from the law currently indexed by the surrounding expression

symbols.name: I_{n,k}
symbols.role: evaluation fold in the two-fold split

symbols.name: delta_0
symbols.role: radius of the local ANM path

symbols.name: b
symbols.def: b = 1 - alpha^2

symbols.name: Z
symbols.role: exogenous parent at the reversible center

symbols.name: E
symbols.def: E ~ N(0,b), independent of Z

symbols.name: delta_n
symbols.def: an arbitrary positive local-curvature sequence indexed by n
symbols.role: moving local alternative in the score-contrast question

symbols.name: r_n
symbols.role: uniform L4 estimability rate for diagonal score-Jacobian functionals

symbols.name: q_n
symbols.role: rare-event probability in the adversarial nuisance perturbation

symbols.name: M_n
symbols.role: amplitude in the adversarial nuisance perturbation

symbols.name: M
symbols.role: fixed tail threshold in the training-random L4 characterization

symbols.name: e
symbols.role: generic nuisance error in a centered-variance comparison

symbols.name: f_delta
symbols.def: f_delta(z) = alpha z + delta g(z)

symbols.name: P_0
symbols.def: Law(Z, alpha Z + E)

symbols.name: P_{h,delta}
symbols.def: P_{h,delta}=P_{+,delta} when h=+ and P_{h,delta}=P_{-,delta} when h=-
symbols.role: generic member of the two-arrow mirror experiment

symbols.name: omega
symbols.def: omega(P_{h,delta})=h for h in {+,-} and 0<|delta|<delta_0; omega is undefined at the common limit P_0
symbols.role: observational-law target on the identified portion of the mirror path

symbols.name: W_delta
symbols.def: W_delta=b^(-1)[-2 alpha g'(X)+(Y-f_delta(X))g''(X)-delta g'(X)^2] under P_{+,delta}
symbols.role: non-leaf score-Jacobian fluctuation along the forward path

symbols.name: p_{h,delta}
symbols.def: Lebesgue density of P_{h,delta}, h in {+,-}

symbols.name: s_P
symbols.def: s_P(x,y) = grad_(x,y) log p(x,y) for a positive C^2 density p of P

symbols.name: J_j(P)
symbols.def: J_j(P) = partial_j s_{P,j}(X,Y) for (X,Y) ~ P and j in {x,y}

symbols.name: V_j(P)
symbols.def: V_j(P) = Var_P[J_j(P)] for j in {x,y}

symbols.name: C_m(delta)
symbols.def: C_m(delta) = min_(h in {+,-}) max_(j in {x,y}) V_j(P_{h,delta})
symbols.role: two-node minimum non-leaf score-Jacobian variance gap

symbols.name: v_h
symbols.def: v_h = partial_delta log p_{h,delta}|_(delta=0), h in {+,-}

symbols.name: c_1
symbols.def: c_1 = b^(-2) Var[2 alpha g'(Z) - E g''(Z)]

symbols.name: Phi
symbols.def: the standard normal distribution function

symbols.name: C_star(delta)
symbols.def: C_star(delta) = -log inf_(t in [0,1]) integral_(R^2) p_{+,delta}(x,y)^t p_{-,delta}(x,y)^(1-t) dx dy

symbols.name: A_n
symbols.def: A_n = integral_(R^2) sqrt(p_{+,delta_n}(x,y)p_{-,delta_n}(x,y)) dx dy = exp(-C_star(delta_n))
symbols.role: Hellinger affinity along the moving mirror experiment

symbols.name: m_n
symbols.def: m_n(x,y)=A_n^(-1)sqrt(p_{+,delta_n}(x,y)p_{-,delta_n}(x,y))

symbols.name: ell_delta
symbols.def: ell_delta(x,y)=log[p_{+,delta}(x,y)/p_{-,delta}(x,y)]

symbols.name: L_n
symbols.def: L_n=sum_(i=1)^n ell_{delta_n}(X_i,Y_i)

symbols.name: T_n
symbols.role: measurable order rules at sample size n

symbols.name: mathsf S_n
symbols.def: mathsf S_n((x_i,y_i)_{i=1}^n)=((y_i,x_i)_{i=1}^n)
symbols.role: coordinate-swap symmetry of the mirror experiment

symbols.name: a_n
symbols.def: a Borel tie breaker satisfying a_n(mathsf S_n w)=-a_n(w) whenever mathsf S_n w != w

symbols.name: pi_{n,delta}^LR
symbols.def: the antisymmetrically tie-broken likelihood-ratio rule for the pair (P_{+,delta}^n,P_{-,delta}^n) in def:mirror-equalizer-rule
symbols.role: delta-indexed equalizer rule for the mirror binary experiment

symbols.name: R_n(delta)
symbols.def: R_n(delta) = inf_(pi in T_n) max_(h in {+,-}) P_{h,delta}^n{pi != h}

symbols.name: D_n
symbols.def: a score-contrast order rule returned by an instantiation of def:crossfitted-score-contrast-template

symbols.name: widehat J_{j,k}
symbols.def: a diagonal score-Jacobian estimate trained on observations outside I_{n,k}

symbols.name: tilde J_{j,k}
symbols.def: rare-event perturbed diagonal score-Jacobian estimate in def:rare-event-nuisance-perturbation

symbols.name: widehat V_{j,k}
symbols.role: foldwise empirical variance of widehat J_{j,k}

symbols.name: K_{+,n}
symbols.def: K_{+,n}=E_{m_n^n}[exp(L_n/2)1{widehat Delta_n<0}]

symbols.name: K_{-,n}
symbols.def: K_{-,n}=E_{m_n^n}[exp(-L_n/2)1{widehat Delta_n>=0}]

symbols.name: G_n
symbols.space: sigma-field on the full data and training randomness
symbols.def: G_n is the joint returned-function sigma-field generated by the two fitted functions returned by the cross-fitted template
symbols.role: conditioning sigma-field for a randomized template instantiation

symbols.name: widehat sigma_n
symbols.role: empirical standard deviation of the foldwise variance contrast

novel.justification: A nondegenerate correlated Gaussian center is the intersection of the two arrow orientations; standardizing both marginals makes swapping X and Y an exact symmetry.
Removing constant and linear components isolates departure from the linear-Gaussian reversible submodel; polynomial and smooth bounded nonlinear mechanisms can always be reparameterized in this way.

The condition excludes affine links and permits smooth quadratic, spline, and sigmoid mechanisms on the Gaussian design; it is the substantive directional-asymmetry restriction, not a condition on a testing distance.

assumptions.condition: There exists eta > 0 such that E exp(eta(|g(Z)|+|g'(Z)|+|g''(Z)|)) < infinity.

novel.justification: This explicit envelope holds for bounded smooth links and for polynomial links of degree at most two when eta is sufficiently small under the Gaussian parent; it supplies a verifiable local tail class without treating a testing conclusion as an assumption.

assumptions.condition: For every n>=2, k in {1,2}, and j in {x,y}, the same single data-measurable map of the observations outside I_{n,k}, independent of h and of the unknown observational law P, returns widehat J_{j,k}; its L4(P_{h,delta_n}) error for J_j(P_{h,delta_n}) is O_{P_{h,delta_n}^n}(r_n), uniformly over (h,j,k) in {+,-} x {x,y} x {1,2}.

maintained.reason: ass:jacobian-estimability is a disclosed high-level cross-fitted L4 nuisance-training rate used only for the conditional feasible-template conclusion in prop:score-contrast-template; the note does not derive it or claim a generic learner.
maintained.open_object: oeq:primitive-tilted-attainability: primitive, verifiable conditions on a concrete two-fold nuisance-training procedure that control the tilted mistake losses remain open.
maintained.separate_object: ass:jacobian-estimability constrains the training-random error of a separate nuisance-training object, not the causal-direction functional omega(P_{h,delta}) or the likelihood and C_m frontier at their conclusion scale.

definitions.name: order-rule collection
definitions.construction: T_n={pi:(R^2)^n -> {+,-}: pi is Borel measurable} for every n in {1,2,...}.

definitions.name: antisymmetric likelihood-ratio equalizer
definitions.construction: For w=((x_i,y_i))_{i=1}^n, let mathsf S_n w=((y_i,x_i))_{i=1}^n and choose a Borel a_n with a_n(mathsf S_n w)=-a_n(w) off the fixed-point set. For every (n,delta) in {1,2,...} x (-delta_0,delta_0), set pi_{n,delta}^LR(w)=+ if product_{i=1}^n p_{+,delta}(x_i,y_i)>product_{i=1}^n p_{-,delta}(x_i,y_i), set pi_{n,delta}^LR(w)=- if the inequality is reversed, and set pi_{n,delta}^LR(w)=a_n(w) on equality.

definitions.name: score-Jacobian variance map
definitions.construction: (s_P,J_j(P),V_j(P))=(grad log p,partial_j s_{P,j}(X,Y),Var_P[J_j(P)]) for every positive C^2 law P on R^2 and j in {x,y}.

definitions.name: local order-testing experiment
definitions.construction: R_n(delta)=inf_{pi in T_n} max_{h in {+,-}} P_{h,delta}^n{pi((X_i,Y_i)_{i=1}^n) != h} for every (n,delta) in {1,2,...} x (-delta_0,delta_0).

definitions.name: cross-fitted score-contrast template
definitions.construction: For n>=2 and any two-fold cross-fitted L4 rate realizers widehat J_{j,k} returned by the same h/P-independent data-measurable training mappings in ass:jacobian-estimability, let I_{n,1}={1,...,floor(n/2)} and I_{n,2}={floor(n/2)+1,...,n}. For each k in {1,2}, train widehat J_{j,k} on the observations outside I_{n,k}; set widehat V_{j,k}=|I_{n,k}|^{-1} sum_{i in I_{n,k}}[widehat J_{j,k}(X_i,Y_i)-|I_{n,k}|^{-1}sum_{ell in I_{n,k}}widehat J_{j,k}(X_ell,Y_ell)]^2, widehat Delta_n=2^{-1}sum_{k=1}^2(widehat V_{x,k}-widehat V_{y,k}), widehat sigma_n^2=2^{-1}sum_{k=1}^2|I_{n,k}|^{-1}sum_{i in I_{n,k}}( [widehat J_{x,k}(X_i,Y_i)-|I_{n,k}|^{-1}sum_{ell in I_{n,k}}widehat J_{x,k}(X_ell,Y_ell)]^2-[widehat J_{y,k}(X_i,Y_i)-|I_{n,k}|^{-1}sum_{ell in I_{n,k}}widehat J_{y,k}(X_ell,Y_ell)]^2-widehat Delta_n)^2, widehat Z_n=sqrt(n)widehat Delta_n/(widehat sigma_n vee n^{-1}), and D_n=+ if widehat Z_n>=0 and D_n=- otherwise.

definitions.name: tilted score-contrast mistake losses
definitions.construction: For every admissible delta_n downarrow 0 with n C_m(delta_n)->infinity, set A_n=integral sqrt(p_{+,delta_n}p_{-,delta_n})=exp(-C_star(delta_n)), m_n=A_n^(-1)sqrt(p_{+,delta_n}p_{-,delta_n}), ell_delta=log(p_{+,delta}/p_{-,delta}), L_n=sum_(i=1)^n ell_{delta_n}(X_i,Y_i), K_{+,n}=E_{m_n^n}[exp(L_n/2)1{widehat Delta_n<0}], and K_{-,n}=E_{m_n^n}[exp(-L_n/2)1{widehat Delta_n>=0}]. Handle: search for primitive, verifiable conditions on a concrete nuisance-training procedure that make log max(K_{+,n},K_{-,n})=o(n C_star(delta_n)), using the returned-function sigma-field G_n rather than an evaluation-independent training field.

definitions.name: rare-event nuisance perturbation
definitions.construction: For an admissible delta_n downarrow 0 satisfying n C_star(delta_n)/log log(n+e)->infinity, set q_n=1/log(n+e) and fix a positive deterministic amplitude schedule M_n up-arrow infinity; both schedules are independent of h, delta_n, and the unknown law P. For each k in {1,2}, select one distinct observation i(k) outside I_{n,k} and set A_{n,k}={X_{i(k)}>Phi^{-1}(1-q_n)}. The A_{n,k} are independent under P_{+,delta_n} and each has probability q_n; under P_{-,delta_n}, each probability tends to zero by uniform local tightness and the diverging threshold. Starting from any common h/P-independent data-measurable rate-realizing maps widehat J_{j,k}, set tilde J_{x,k}=widehat J_{x,k} and tilde J_{y,k}=widehat J_{y,k} off A_{n,k}; on A_{n,k}, set tilde J_{x,k}=0 and tilde J_{y,k}=M_n x. The perturbed maps are the same common h/P-independent data-measurable mappings under both hypotheses. Insert (tilde J_{x,k},tilde J_{y,k}) into def:crossfitted-score-contrast-template.

estimand_functional: omega(P_{h,delta})=+ iff V_y(P_{h,delta})=0<V_x(P_{h,delta}), and omega(P_{h,delta})=- iff V_x(P_{h,delta})=0<V_y(P_{h,delta}), for 0<|delta|<delta_0

sampling_model.treatment: none; the causal object is the directed structural edge
sampling_model.nuisance_estimation: thm:local-geometry, thm:local-minimax-frontier, and thm:local-minimax-elbow use the indexed densities. Conditional on maintained ass:jacobian-estimability, the feasible rung is the newly defined cross-fitted score-contrast template using the same hypothesis-independent, training-random rate-realizing two-fold diagonal score-Jacobian maps under both hypotheses: in the supercritical regime n C_m(delta_n)->infinity, r_n=o(sqrt(C_m(delta_n))) gives sign consistency. Primitive verification remains oeq:primitive-tilted-attainability. Its weak L4-in-probability premise and the tilted algebra are supporting diagnostics, not an exponent claim for any published SCORE implementation.

statements.statement: For every sufficiently small delta != 0, P_{+,delta} is the nonlinear additive-Gaussian ANM X->Y, P_{-,delta} is the nonlinear additive-Gaussian ANM Y->X, V_y(P_{+,delta})=V_x(P_{-,delta})=0, and V_x(P_{+,delta})=V_y(P_{-,delta})=C_m(delta)>0. As delta -> 0, C_m(delta)=c_1 delta^2+o(delta^2), c_1>0, and C_star(delta)=(c_2/8)delta^2+o(delta^2)=(c_2/(8c_1))C_m(delta)+o(C_m(delta)), with c_2>0.
statements.justification: This theorem derives the observational information scale from SCORE's native population gap rather than assuming a testing separation.
statements.gap: Zhu et al. relate a curvature margin to a sufficient SCORE guarantee, while Hoyer et al. and Peters et al. give qualitative directional asymmetry. Montagna et al.'s ScoreSort studies bivariate score-component-variance ordering on its score-sortable subclass, and Montagna et al.'s broader score framework gives population identifiability conditions; neither relates SCORE's diagonal score-Jacobian variance C_m(delta) to Chernoff information at the linear-Gaussian boundary.
statements.consumer: It calibrates the likelihood frontier, the local-minimax elbow, and the cross-fitted score-contrast template against Zhu, Locatello, and Cevher's curvature-margin SCORE guarantee on the same mirror experiment.

statements.statement: For each fixed sufficiently small delta != 0, R_n(delta)=exp{-n[C_star(delta)+o_n(1)]} as n -> infinity. Consequently, lim_(delta downarrow 0) lim_(n -> infinity) [-log R_n(delta)]/[n C_m(delta)] = c_2/(8c_1), so the optimal binary order-testing exponent in the mirror experiment is linear in C_m(delta).
statements.justification: The local geometry becomes operational only after the binary-testing risk is shown to inherit its Chernoff scale.
statements.gap: Chernoff characterizes generic simple-versus-simple product testing, while Zhu et al.'s Theorem 2 analyzes a score-estimation upper guarantee. Montagna et al. analyze score-sortability and score-based identifiability rather than the product-measure risk of this opposite-order Gaussian-ANM pair; neither result states a likelihood frontier indexed by SCORE's C_m(delta).
statements.consumer: It is the likelihood benchmark for the local-minimax elbow and for the score-contrast template, and it calibrates Zhu, Locatello, and Cevher's sufficient curvature-margin guarantee on the same mirror experiment.

statements.statement: For every (n,delta), the antisymmetrically tie-broken likelihood-ratio rule pi_{n,delta}^LR satisfies R_n(delta)=P_{+,delta}^n{pi_{n,delta}^LR != +}=P_{-,delta}^n{pi_{n,delta}^LR != -}. Along every delta_n downarrow 0, E_{m_n}[ell_{delta_n}(X,Y)^2]=O(delta_n^2). If n C_m(delta_n)->rho in [0,infinity), then R_n(delta_n)->Phi(-0.5 sqrt(rho c_2/c_1)); if n C_m(delta_n)->infinity, then [-log R_n(delta_n)]/[n C_star(delta_n)]->1 and [-log R_n(delta_n)]/[n C_m(delta_n)]->c_2/(8c_1). Both limits are pointwise for the fixed admissible mirror path.
statements.justification: The elbow gives the nondegenerate local decision limit and the supercritical error exponent between the unrecoverable center and exponentially recoverable regime.
statements.gap: Chernoff supplies a fixed-alternative exponent, while Hoyer et al. and Peters et al. identify the reversible boundary only qualitatively. Neither provides the equalizer-based moving-alternative risk limit indexed by SCORE's C_m(delta) for this mirror ANM path.
statements.consumer: It converts a reported score-gap scale into the exact pointwise local power boundary and supercritical likelihood benchmark for choosing between the two causal orders.

statements.statement: At delta=0, P_{+,0}=P_{-,0}=P_0 and R_n(0)=1/2 for every n.
statements.justification: The proposed local frontier must collapse to the known linear-Gaussian observational equivalence case.
statements.gap: Hoyer et al. identify linear-Gaussian models as reversible, but do not quantify how the finite-sample directional error leaves that boundary.
statements.consumer: It verifies that the local lower bound is anchored at a genuine nonidentification point rather than at a poorly conditioned but identified model.

statements.statement: Under ass:jacobian-estimability, the following conditional score-contrast result holds. Under P_{+,delta}, J_y(P_{+,delta})=-b^(-1) and J_x(P_{+,delta})=-b^(-1)+delta W_delta; under P_{-,delta} the two identities are swapped. For every U,e in L^2(P), |Var_P(U+e)-Var_P(U)| <= 2 sqrt(Var_P(U) Var_P(e))+Var_P(e). Hence, conditionally on each training fold, an L4 nuisance error e with ||e||_{L4(P_{h,delta_n})}=O_P(r_n) changes the non-leaf centered variance by O_P(|delta_n|r_n+r_n^2) and the leaf centered variance by O_P(r_n^2). For every instantiation D_n of def:crossfitted-score-contrast-template using the common rate realizers in ass:jacobian-estimability, if delta_n downarrow 0, n C_m(delta_n)->infinity, and r_n=o(sqrt(C_m(delta_n))), then max_{h in {+,-}}P_{h,delta_n}^n{D_n != h}->0.
statements.justification: Conditional on maintained ass:jacobian-estimability for common hypothesis-independent training maps, centering annihilates the constant leaf baseline, so the displayed L4 scale is sufficient for supercritical sign consistency of the newly defined template.
statements.gap: Zhu et al. provide a sufficient score-estimation guarantee, but neither their workflow nor any cited result analyzes this new cross-fitted diagonal-Jacobian contrast with the centered-variance calculation.
statements.consumer: Conditional on maintained ass:jacobian-estimability for common training maps, n C_m(delta_n)->infinity, and r_n=o(sqrt(C_m(delta_n))), it supplies a concrete sufficient scale for consistent directional decisions by this template; primitive verification remains oeq:primitive-tilted-attainability, and no likelihood exponent is claimed for published SCORE implementations.

statements.statement: Which primitive, verifiable conditions on a concrete two-fold nuisance-training procedure imply log max(K_{+,n},K_{-,n})=o(n C_star(delta_n)) along an admissible sequence delta_n downarrow 0 with n C_m(delta_n)->infinity?
statements.justification: The tilted representation supplies a precise diagnostic, but a practitioner needs checkable training conditions that control it.
statements.gap: No cited score-based causal-order result translates a concrete nuisance-training algorithm into the tilted mistake-loss requirement for this mirror experiment.
statements.consumer: It supplies a route from the mirror likelihood benchmark to an implementable score-based order detector whose exponent can be certified.

tldr: For the explicitly defined two-node mirror additive-Gaussian path (P_{+,delta},P_{-,delta}), the retained baseline maps SCORE’s diagonal score-Jacobian variance gap to Chernoff information and gives the pointwise fixed- and moving-alternative likelihood frontier. The requested v20 field upgrade was a composite unknown-mechanism frontier over a Gaussian-Hermite Sobolev class with s>2. It cannot be constructed cleanly from those primitives: Sobolev control alone supplies no uniform exponential/multiplication remainder for the score-Jacobian calculations, but imposing such a uniform envelope restricts the high-frequency packets that determine the proposed modulus. Thus no matched modulus, block-mixture lower bound, or universal quadratic detector is claimed. Conditional common-map score-contrast sign consistency and the template-only L4 diagnostic remain supporting baseline targets; primitive trainer verification remains open.

project_justification.gap: The retained mirror experiment identifies the local likelihood scale of SCORE’s native gap for one fixed mechanism. The proposed field extension requires a composite unknown-mechanism class in which the same score gap controls the least-favourable high-frequency alternatives and a universal detector. A Gaussian-Hermite Sobolev radius with s>2 does not by itself supply the uniform nonlinear remainder required for that claim.
project_justification.niche: The obstruction is structural rather than a missing concentration inequality: numerical tail/multiplication control is necessary for a uniform exact-ANM score calculation, yet it changes the feasible high-frequency packets unless its interaction with the Sobolev radius is itself characterized.
project_justification.fill: This rebuild preserves the valid pointwise mirror-path package and records the failed composite construction explicitly. It does not relabel the pointwise pair as a uniform adaptive minimax frontier or assume the requested modulus, mixture separation, or detector Gram bound.

related_work: Rolland et al. (rolland2022score) establish the diagonal score-Jacobian leaf characterization on which the population functional rests; they do not map that variance to likelihood information at a reversible boundary. Zhu, Locatello, and Cevher (zhu2023sample, Section 3 and Theorem 2) turn SCORE into a sufficient finite-sample curvature-margin guarantee, but do not give the mirror C_m(delta)-to-Chernoff map or the local minimax elbow. Hoyer et al. (hoyer2008nonlinear) and Peters et al. (peters2014anm) establish qualitative ANM directional asymmetry and exceptional reversibility, rather than a pointwise local testing modulus. Kpotufe, Sgouritsa, Janzing, and Schölkopf (kpotufe2014, Sections 1.2 and 5) prove residual-independence ANM direction consistency without a general finite-sample rate, so their result does not yield this score-gap/Chernoff calibration. Montagna, Noceti, Rosasco, Zhang, and Locatello (montagna2023das, Sections 3--3.3 and Conclusion) use score-Jacobian derivatives for nonlinear additive-Gaussian graph recovery and scalable pruning; DAS does not analyze a mirror two-simple-hypothesis likelihood experiment. Montagna, Noceti, Rosasco, and Locatello (montagna2023shortcuts, Sections 3.1 and 4) characterize score-sortability and ScoreSort through score-component variances, a different statistic that supplies no diagonal-Jacobian-gap Chernoff map near reversibility. Montagna, Faller, Bloebaum, Kirschbaum, and Locatello (montagna2025roof, Sections 4.1--4.3 and Appendix E.3) give score-based identification and AdaScore across linear, nonlinear, and latent settings, not a local likelihood efficiency analysis of this template. Montagna et al. (montagna2023nogam) document Gaussian-score inversions under non-Gaussian noise and motivate a separate generic-noise frontier; it informs but is not compared against this Gaussian mirror result. Chernoff (chernoff1952measure) supplies the fixed-simple-hypothesis product-measure exponent, not the ANM geometry that indexes it by C_m(delta). Koehler, Heckett, and Risteski (koehler2023efficiency) show that score matching can lose information relative to likelihood for density parameters; they motivate but are not refuted by the template-specific search for a concrete trainer that controls the tilted mistake losses. The halted composite upgrade does not alter these comparisons: none of the cited papers supplies the missing uniform interaction between an s>2 Gaussian-Hermite Sobolev radius, a numerical score-Jacobian remainder envelope, and a direction-symmetric high-frequency detector.
technical_internal_limitation: For the newly defined template only, def:rare-event-nuisance-perturbation provides an explicit diagnostic on local sequences satisfying n C_star(delta_n)/log log(n+e)->infinity, equivalently n C_m(delta_n)/log log(n+e)->infinity by thm:local-geometry. The preliminary bookkeeping identities are P_{+,delta_n}^n{D_n=-}=exp(-n C_star(delta_n)) K_{+,n} and P_{-,delta_n}^n{D_n=+}=exp(-n C_star(delta_n)) K_{-,n}. Its deterministic schedules are q_n=1/log(n+e) and M_n, both independent of h, delta_n, and the unknown law P; the distinct-fold triggers are independent under P_{+,delta_n} with probability q_n, while under P_{-,delta_n} their probabilities tend to zero by uniform local tightness and the diverging threshold. The perturbed maps remain the same common h/P-independent data-measurable mappings under both hypotheses. The training-random L4 containment is Pr{||tilde J_{j,k}-J_j(P_{h,delta_n})||_4>M r_n} <= Pr(A_{n,k})+Pr{||widehat J_{j,k}-J_j(P_{h,delta_n})||_4>M r_n}, and it remains O_P because Pr(A_{n,k})->0 under both hypotheses. The construction has P_{+,delta_n}^n{D_n=-}>=q_n^2, so 0<=-log P_{+,delta_n}^n{D_n=-}/[n C_star(delta_n)]<=2 log log(n+e)/[n C_star(delta_n)]->0 and log K_{+,n}>=n C_star(delta_n)-2 log log(n+e). Thus L4 O_P control does not logically imply a positive exponent fraction uniformly over admissible instantiations of this new template, as an explicit counterexample on this faster admissible subsequence. This is not a theorem contribution, an impossibility claim for SCORE, or a claim about any published estimator.
honest_scope: The likelihood frontier and elbow are pointwise for the explicitly defined two-node mirror Gaussian-ANM path. The causal-direction functional is defined only for 0<|delta|<delta_0; P_0 is solely the common limiting law. Conditional on maintained ass:jacobian-estimability for the same hypothesis-independent training maps under both laws, the newly defined score-contrast template has sign consistency only along sequences with n C_m(delta_n)->infinity and r_n=o(sqrt(C_m(delta_n))). Its training-random L4 nuisance control does not by itself determine an exponent: the technical internal limitation gives a deterministic common-map counterexample only on the faster diagnostic subsequence n C_star(delta_n)/log log(n+e)->infinity, equivalently n C_m(delta_n)/log log(n+e)->infinity. Primitive verification remains oeq:primitive-tilted-attainability. No global uniform nonlinear-ANM constant is claimed because c_1 and c_2 have no lower envelopes over the admissible mechanisms. In particular, this core does not define or claim a composite Gaussian-Hermite Sobolev minimax frontier: such a claim needs a separately proved interaction between the Sobolev radius, a uniform score-Jacobian remainder envelope, and the allowed high-frequency packets. The result is not a score-sortability theorem for score-component variances, a broad latent-variable score-identification theorem, or a neural-score implementation result.

comparator_promise_table.comparator_claim: Provides a sufficient finite-sample correctness bound for SCORE topological ordering under a curvature margin.
Shows that nonlinear additive-Gaussian leaves are identified by constancy of a diagonal score-Jacobian entry.
Shows generic directional asymmetry of nonlinear additive-noise models and identifies linear-Gaussian reversibility as an exceptional case.
Proves population DAG identifiability for continuous additive-noise models under restricted conditions.
Characterizes the exponential error rate of simple-versus-simple product-measure testing by Chernoff information.
Characterizes efficiency gaps between score matching and likelihood for density estimation through distributional geometry.
Shows Gaussian-score edge-inversion risk under non-Gaussian additive noise and proposes NoGAM.
Uses the score Jacobian to recover and orient edges and to reduce pruning cost in nonlinear additive-Gaussian DAGs.
Characterizes bivariate score-sortability and analyzes ScoreSort, which orders by score-component variances on a subclass overlapping nonlinear ANMs.
Gives score-based causal-identifiability conditions and the empirically evaluated AdaScore framework across linear, nonlinear, and latent-variable models.
Establishes nonparametric consistency conditions for residual-independence additive-noise direction procedures and does not give a general finite-sample causal-direction rate.

Chernoff, H. (1952). A Measure of Asymptotic Efficiency for Tests of a Hypothesis Based on the Sum of Observations. Annals of Mathematical Statistics 23, 493-507.

Peters, J., Mooij, J. M., Janzing, D., and Schölkopf, B. (2014). Causal Discovery with Continuous Additive Noise Models. Journal of Machine Learning Research 15, 2009-2053.
Koehler, F., Heckett, A., and Risteski, A. (2023). Statistical Efficiency of Score Matching: The View from Isoperimetry. ICLR.
Montagna, F., Noceti, N., Rosasco, L., Zhang, K., and Locatello, F. (2023). Causal Discovery with Score Matching on Additive Models with Arbitrary Noise. CLeaR.

Montagna, F., Noceti, N., Rosasco, L., Zhang, K., and Locatello, F. (2023). Scalable Causal Discovery with Score Matching. Proceedings of the Second Conference on Causal Learning and Reasoning, PMLR 213, 752-771.
Montagna, F., Noceti, N., Rosasco, L., and Locatello, F. (2023). Shortcuts for Causal Discovery of Nonlinear Models by Score Matching. arXiv:2310.14246.

Montagna, F., Faller, P. M., Blobaum, P., Kirschbaum, E., and Locatello, F. (2025). Score Matching through the Roof: Linear, Nonlinear, and Latent Variables Causal Discovery. Proceedings of the Fourth Conference on Causal Learning and Reasoning, PMLR 275, 552-605.
Kpotufe, S., Sgouritsa, E., Janzing, D., and Schölkopf, B. (2014). Consistency of Causal Inference under the Additive Noise Model. ICML, PMLR 32(2), 478-486.

literature_map:
1. Rolland et al. (2022) prove that nonlinear additive-Gaussian leaf nodes have constant diagonal score-Jacobian entries; their variance map yields the population SCORE order rule.
2. Zhu, Locatello, and Cevher (2023, Section 3 and Theorem 2) give a finite-sample SCORE topological-order guarantee through score estimation and a curvature-related margin, not a likelihood-minimax calibration.
3. Montagna et al. (2023, Sections 3--3.3 and Conclusion) use the score Jacobian to identify and orient edges and to reduce the cost of graph pruning in nonlinear additive-Gaussian DAGs; DAS does not analyze a local opposite-order experiment, its Chernoff information, or a SCORE-gap error exponent.
4. Montagna et al. (2023, Sections 3.1 and 4) characterize bivariate score-sortability and show that ScoreSort's score-component-variance rule can be more sample-efficient than SCORE on a score-sortable subclass; it does not use diagonal score-Jacobian variance or derive a near-reversible testing exponent.
5. Montagna et al. (2025, Sections 4.1--4.3 and Appendix E.3) give score-based causal-identifiability conditions and an empirically evaluated AdaScore framework for linear, nonlinear, and latent-variable models; it does not specify the mirror Gaussian-ANM likelihood experiment or compare a score detector with Chernoff information.
6. Kpotufe et al. (2014, Sections 1.2 and 5) establish nonparametric consistency for residual-independence ANM direction procedures but explicitly do not obtain general finite-sample convergence rates.
7. Hoyer et al. (2008, Section 3) show that linear-Gaussian ANMs are reversibly representable while generic nonlinear ANMs are directionally asymmetric.
8. Peters et al. (2014) extend population ANM identifiability to DAGs, but do not quantify distance to the reversible exceptional family.
9. Chernoff (1952) supplies the optimal simple-versus-simple product-testing exponent required by the two-order experiment.
10. Koehler, Heckett, and Risteski (2023, Sections 4 and 8) show that score matching may be efficient or inefficient relative to likelihood for density estimation, not causal-order testing.
11. Montagna et al. (2023, Section 7) establish Gaussian-SCORE inversion risk under non-Gaussian noise and motivate a different generic-noise project.
12. The local structure-identification frontier at the Gaussian reversible center is therefore the mapping from SCORE's diagonal-Jacobian gap to observational information, followed by the unresolved feasibility of retaining that information with a score rule.
prior_work_summary: The active directory contains only rejected skeletons and no prior SCORE-specific proposal whose theorem subsumes this local two-point experiment. The accepted LiNGAM direction-minimality entry concerns non-Gaussian latent cumulants, not nonlinear Gaussian score-Jacobian geometry. It is not used as a comparator.
seeds: Mirror opposite-arrow ANM paths yield a Chernoff/minimax order-error exponent indexed by SCORE's score-variance gap C_m.
A local modulus atlas classifies curvature degeneracies for which score gap and Hellinger separation vanish at distinct orders.
A necessary-and-sufficient local efficiency criterion determines when a score-based leaf statistic attains the likelihood order-testing exponent.
A generic-noise analogue of C_m yields matching NoGAM order-recovery bounds and exposes Gaussian-SCORE inversion regimes.
An iff characterization identifies precisely when a diagonal score-Jacobian leaf certificate remains valid beyond Gaussian innovations.
A decomposition of raw SCORE error separates score-estimation loss from intrinsic order-testing information and identifies their divergence frontier.
A Le Cam local asymptotic experiment for ANM direction gives a non-Gaussian limit law for near-reversible causal-order decisions.
A uniform lower bound over smooth nonlinear ANMs shows that every order estimator needs n C_m observations away from affine degeneracy.

seed_details.one_line: Mirror opposite-arrow ANM paths yield a Chernoff/minimax order-error exponent indexed by SCORE's score-variance gap C_m.

anchor_paper.author: Zhu et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Finite-sample SCORE correctness is bounded through a curvature margin.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: PASS: the named focal object is a derived Chernoff-to-score-gap map, not generic consistency.
seed_details.referee_objection: Immediate from Chernoff because this is only a binary test.
seed_details.rebuttal: Chernoff alone does not construct opposite-order ANMs, compute their SCORE gap, or establish that the two local quantities are proportional.

adversarial_referee.instance: A single pair cannot be a minimax statement over ANMs.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: It is explicitly a least-favorable subexperiment lower bound, not a global-class equality.

adversarial_referee.instance: Zhu's bound may not be quadratic in C_m.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The core compares only its sufficient margin-based guarantee; no formula-level strict equality is attributed.

adversarial_referee.instance: The reverse law might not be an ANM.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The construction generates P_- directly by the swapped additive-Gaussian structural equation.

seed_details.one_line: A local modulus atlas classifies curvature degeneracies for which score gap and Hellinger separation vanish at distinct orders.

anchor_paper.author: Hoyer et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Generic nonlinear ANMs are directionally asymmetric while exceptional reversible cases remain.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: PASS: its object is a derived divergence frontier across Hermite orders.
seed_details.referee_objection: A Taylor expansion catalogue is bookkeeping.
seed_details.rebuttal: It survives only if it supplies a necessary-and-sufficient degeneracy classification, which is presently technically underdeveloped.

adversarial_referee.instance: Necessity may fail outside analytic mechanisms.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Risk is material; do not select absent an analytic classification.

adversarial_referee.instance: No estimator or identification object is named.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The modulus itself is focal, but this weakens practical leverage.

adversarial_referee.instance: A Hermite example is hand-tuned.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Must be uniform over a smooth tangent class.

seed_details.one_line: A necessary-and-sufficient local efficiency criterion determines when a score-based leaf statistic attains the likelihood order-testing exponent.

anchor_paper.author: Koehler et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Score matching can be comparable to or less efficient than likelihood depending on distributional geometry.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: PASS: it asks for an observable equality frontier, not a broad efficiency slogan.
seed_details.referee_objection: An L4 nuisance rate alone cannot determine an exact large-deviation exponent.
seed_details.rebuttal: The score-contrast template separates sign consistency from the tilted-loss diagnostic and the open search for primitive attainability conditions.

adversarial_referee.instance: Only a sufficient condition is likely available.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Land; retain only as an open estimation rung.

adversarial_referee.instance: The anchor concerns density estimation, not causal order.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The order specialization is exactly the unresolved transfer and cannot be claimed as an extension.

adversarial_referee.instance: No local family makes efficiency calculable.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The mirror family in the chosen seed supplies it.

seed_details.one_line: A generic-noise analogue of C_m yields matching NoGAM order-recovery bounds and exposes Gaussian-SCORE inversion regimes.

anchor_paper.author: Montagna et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Gaussian-score inference can invert edges under non-Gaussian innovations; NoGAM is consistent.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: PASS only if a concrete generic-noise separation functional is derived.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a wholesale new theory with no canonical margin.
seed_details.rebuttal: Lands for the present run: no viable canonical local center or detector has been established.

adversarial_referee.instance: No opposite-order generic-noise family is specified.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: lands; drop from candidates.

adversarial_referee.instance: A NoGAM upper bound would not identify the minimax frontier.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: lands; drop from candidates.

adversarial_referee.instance: NoGAM changes the statistic, so C_m is irrelevant.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: lands; drop from candidates.

seed_details.one_line: An iff characterization identifies precisely when a diagonal score-Jacobian leaf certificate remains valid beyond Gaussian innovations.

anchor_paper.author: Rolland et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Diagonal score-Jacobian constancy identifies leaves under nonlinear additive Gaussian noise.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: PASS: an iff condition is a focal object.
seed_details.referee_objection: The answer is immediate: Gaussianity is necessary.
seed_details.rebuttal: It is not immediate, but the requisite restricted-ANM functional equation is broad and lacks a sharp practicable condition.

adversarial_referee.instance: Necessity needs all innovation laws, not a counterexample.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: High feasibility risk; not selected.

adversarial_referee.instance: Rolland only conjectures broader score reading.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The future-work question supports, but does not prove, an iff frontier.

adversarial_referee.instance: No finite-sample consumer is present.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Would require an M8 rung.

seed_details.one_line: A decomposition of raw SCORE error separates score-estimation loss from intrinsic order-testing information and identifies their divergence frontier.

anchor_paper.author: Zhu et al.
anchor_paper.claim: SCORE order recovery follows from score-estimation control and a curvature margin.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: PASS only with an explicit raw-SCORE error decomposition and a divergence threshold.
seed_details.referee_objection: This merely restates the proof of Zhu et al.
seed_details.rebuttal: It would need a new exact error functional; without it, the objection lands.

adversarial_referee.instance: Upper-bound decomposition cannot show a loss.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Needs a matched lower bound for raw SCORE.

adversarial_referee.instance: Raw SCORE includes neural training randomness.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Must define the exact procedure before comparison.

adversarial_referee.instance: No correction is offered.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The selected template proves only L4 sign consistency and isolates exponent recovery as an exponential-stability question.

anchor_paper.author: Peters et al.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: PASS only if the limiting experiment has a distinct decision consequence.
seed_details.referee_objection: LAN is automatic from smooth densities and adds no causal content.
seed_details.rebuttal: The objection largely lands unless paired with the score-gap mapping; it is absorbed by the chosen Chernoff seed.

adversarial_referee.instance: It is limit theory without a structural focal object.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use it as a proof device, not an angle.

adversarial_referee.instance: Peters has no local asymptotic claim.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The gap is real but too generic.

adversarial_referee.instance: A single path gives no universal LAN theory.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Would need a tangent class.

seed_details.one_line: A uniform lower bound over smooth nonlinear ANMs shows that every order estimator needs n C_m observations away from affine degeneracy.

anchor_paper.author: Zhu et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Finite-sample SCORE success worsens as the curvature margin shrinks.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: PASS only if C_m controls an explicitly defined nondegenerate class rather than one pair.
seed_details.referee_objection: C_m alone cannot control all ANM tangent directions.
seed_details.rebuttal: Likely lands: additional tangent geometry is needed, so the two-point result is the defensible first theorem.

adversarial_referee.instance: A two-point bound cannot establish uniform class sharpness.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The selected theorem makes only the valid subexperiment claim.

adversarial_referee.instance: A uniform lower bound does not show SCORE attains it.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Feasible attainability is an open separate rung.

adversarial_referee.instance: The class has no common curvature normalization.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: lands; do not select.

novelty_justification.repo_axis: No active or accepted CausalSmith core covers SCORE's nonlinear additive-Gaussian score-Jacobian gap. The nearest accepted LiNGAM direction-minimality proposal studies cumulants and non-Gaussian latent structure, so neither its target nor its local information geometry represents this kernel.
novelty_justification.published_axis: The retained pointwise mirror experiment remains distinct from the named SCORE and ANM comparators. The proposed v20 novelty—an unknown-mechanism composite score-gap modulus with a matching universal detector—cannot be claimed because its required uniform class geometry is not derived from the permitted s>2 Sobolev and envelope primitives. No published comparison is tightened by the obstruction notice.

literature_checklist.author: Zhu et al.
literature_checklist.one_line: First finite-sample SCORE topological-order guarantee, with curvature margin but no minimax converse.

literature_checklist.author: Rolland et al.
literature_checklist.one_line: Population diagonal score-Jacobian constancy characterizes leaves in nonlinear additive-Gaussian ANMs.

literature_checklist.author: Chernoff
literature_checklist.one_line: Supplies the simple-versus-simple product-measure error exponent used after the local ANM expansion.

literature_checklist.author: Hoyer et al.
literature_checklist.one_line: Establishes qualitative nonlinear ANM directional asymmetry and isolates the reversible linear-Gaussian special case.

literature_checklist.author: Peters et al.
literature_checklist.one_line: Gives continuous-ANM population DAG identifiability without a local distance-to-reversibility modulus.

literature_checklist.author: Koehler et al.
literature_checklist.one_line: Shows score matching may have an efficiency gap relative to likelihood in density estimation.

literature_checklist.author: Kpotufe et al.
literature_checklist.one_line: Proves consistency for residual-independence ANM direction procedures while explicitly leaving general finite-sample rates unresolved.

literature_checklist.author: Montagna et al.
literature_checklist.one_line: Shows Gaussian-score edge inversion under non-Gaussian innovations and motivates a separate generic-noise frontier.

literature_checklist.author: Montagna et al.
literature_checklist.one_line: Uses the score Jacobian's second derivatives for nonlinear additive-Gaussian graph recovery and scalable pruning, but not a local score-gap/Chernoff order frontier.

literature_checklist.author: Montagna et al.
literature_checklist.one_line: Characterizes bivariate score-sortability and ScoreSort's score-component-variance ordering; it does not supply a diagonal-Jacobian-gap Chernoff frontier.

literature_checklist.author: Montagna et al.
literature_checklist.one_line: Develops score-based identification and AdaScore across linear, nonlinear, and latent settings, but not local likelihood efficiency for a mirror ANM order test.

soundness_self_review.SC1_hypotheses_in_assumptions: PASS -- all regularity, normalization, and nonlinearity restrictions are standalone law/mechanism conditions.
soundness_self_review.SC2_objects_defined: PASS -- all symbols used in assumptions and statements are declared; omega is defined only on the identified nonzero-curvature portion of the mirror path, while P_0 is a limiting law only. U, e, W_delta, M, the Hellinger tilt m_n, and the returned-function sigma-field G_n make the variance, tail, and tilted-loss calculations explicit.
soundness_self_review.SC3_corollaries_reduce: PASS -- prop:linear-reduction gives the known linear-Gaussian reversible case.
soundness_self_review.SC4_intro_kernel_matches_section8: PASS WITH PIVOT NOTICE -- the retained tldr matches the five pointwise baseline targets and the open template-attainability question; it explicitly does not promote them to the unconstructed composite field frontier.
soundness_self_review.SC5_consumer_named: PASS -- every result names an order-recovery or procedure-selection consumer.
soundness_self_review.SC6_comparator_promise: PASS -- each named published comparator is matched or explicitly downgraded in the comparator table.
soundness_self_review.SC12_open_ended_question_discipline: PASS -- oeq:primitive-tilted-attainability uses the explicit tilted-loss handle in def:score-contrast-exponent-handle, without committing a candidate training condition.
soundness_self_review.SC13_inference_conjecture_discipline: PASS -- no universal learner, high-frequency modulus, mixture lower bound, or coefficient-one efficiency claim is emitted. The unresolved composite construction is reported as a needs-pivot obstruction rather than encoded as an assumption.
soundness_self_review.SC14_composite_rebuild: NEEDS-PIVOT -- for a Hermite shell theta_K H_K, the stated Sobolev condition permits |theta_K|=R(1+K)^(-s/2), giving the guide C_m asymptotic to delta^2 R^2 K^(2-s) and C_star asymptotic to delta^2 R^2 K^(-s). However normalized Hermite shells violate any fixed numerical exponential envelope on g, g prime, and g double-prime. Replacing them by bounded packets requires a separate uniform packet construction and uniform exact-ANM remainder bound; these are precisely the unproved components that determine whether the guide survives. No class or theorem is defined by assuming that survival.

construction_rebuild.requested_focal_object: A composite, unknown-mechanism Gaussian-Hermite Sobolev class indexed by a moving SCORE margin C_m>=c, with a uniform C_m-to-C_star modulus, a block-mixture lower bound, and a matching direction-symmetric quadratic detector.
construction_rebuild.attempted_start: For a formal Hermite shell g_K=theta_K H_K with theta_K=R(1+K)^(-s/2), the exact score-Jacobian leading term has scale C_m(delta,g_K) proportional to delta^2 R^2 K^(2-s), while the local tangent/Chernoff scale is proportional to delta^2 R^2 K^(-s). Eliminating K at the local-radius boundary gives the proposed guide C_star proportional to R^(-4/(s-2)) C_m^(s/(s-2)).
construction_rebuild.sanity_reduction: This shell calculation is only a guide, not a class construction: the normalized Hermite polynomial has unbounded exponential moments of |g_K(Z)| (and of its derivatives) at every positive envelope parameter once K>=3. It therefore lies outside the required numerical envelope and cannot witness a uniform exact-ANM theorem.
construction_rebuild.blocking_step: A bounded high-frequency packet must be constructed and shown simultaneously to have Hermite-Sobolev radius R, the same C_m and C_star scales, a fixed numerical multiplication/tail envelope, and a uniform nonlinear remainder. Those properties are not implied by s>2 and cannot be inserted as assumptions without assuming the sought modulus. Without them, the requested block mixture and matching universal detector lack a well-defined least-favourable class.
